claim: In the June 25, 2024 Democratic primary in New York's 16th Congressional District, the United Democracy Project — the super PAC affiliated with the American Israel Public Affairs Committee (AIPAC) — spent approximately $14.5 million to defeat the incumbent Rep. Jamaal Bowman, a vocal critic of the Israeli military campaign in Gaza, in support of his challenger George Latimer. The total spending in the race exceeded $23 million in support of Latimer, making it the most expensive U.S. House primary race in U.S. history. Latimer won the primary by approximately 17 percentage points. Reporting by The Intercept and Mondoweiss documented that the United Democracy Project had drawn substantially on Republican-aligned donors to fund the spending in a Democratic primary. AIPAC's broader 2023-2024 election-cycle spending — through its conventional PAC, the United Democracy Project super PAC, and affiliated independent-expenditure committees — exceeded $100 million, the largest single-cycle spend by a foreign-policy-focused political committee in U.S. history.

sources.label: The Times of Israel — “AIPAC leads unprecedented $14.5 million campaign against Bowman in NY primary.”
The Intercept — “AIPAC's Next Top Target? Rep. Jamaal Bowman” (May 3, 2024).
Mondoweiss — “Latimer defeats Bowman with $15 million worth of help from AIPAC” (June 26, 2024).
OpenSecrets / FEC — United Democracy Project independent-expenditure filings, 2024 election cycle.

notes: OpenSecrets / FEC is the canonical primary record on independent-expenditure totals. The Times of Israel piece is a useful Israeli-press secondary. Latimer's actual margin of victory ranged from 16.5 to 17.4 percentage points across reporting sources.
